steps.title: Spot the pattern the student can emphasize
steps.content: The defining signal: two critics (The Economist and O Magazine) both emphasize storytelling and narrative quality, while the Missoulian emphasizes something different (monumental achievement). This reveals a shared strength the student can highlight.

steps.title: Apply this pattern to the choices
steps.content:
Choice C — Critics praised Treuer's book for its compelling narrative, with O, The Oprah Magazine calling it "a marvel of research and storytelling" and The Economist likewise writing that "Treuer's storytelling skills shine" and that the book is an "elegant handling of [a] complex narrative." does this by opening with “Critics praised Treuer's book for its compelling narrative” to unify the shared element, then providing two specific examples—both highlighting storytelling—with “likewise” to signal the parallel.
  • A mentions the book was widely reviewed without capturing the similarity
  • B uses only one critic's view
  • D explicitly contrasts how critics differed
.

The paired praise from two publications is correct.
